title: podman compose is ignoring dockerfile_inline
body:
### Issue Description

Running `podman compose --file docker-compose.yml up` ignores dockerfile_inline and instead uses the local Dockerfile. If no Dockerfile is present, the following error is displayed `unable to prepare context: unable to evaluate symlinks in Dockerfile path: CreateFile foo\Dockerfile: The system cannot find the file specified.`.

docker-compose.yml:
```
services:
  caddy:
    image: foo/caddy:latest
    build:
      dockerfile_inline: |
        FROM caddy:builder AS builder

        RUN \
          xcaddy build \
            --with github.com/caddyserver/transform-encoder

        FROM caddy

        COPY --from=builder /usr/bin/caddy /usr/bin/caddy
    pull_policy: build

```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman compose --file docker-compose.yml up


### Describe the results you received

See above

### Describe the results you expected

`dockerfile_inline` is used for the build instead of the Dockerfile in the folder

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

`podman compose --version` returns `Docker Compose version v2.40.2-desktop.1`

I'm using Windows 11 Pro 24H2 build 26100.6899 and Windows Subsystems for Linux 2.6.2.0.

### Additional information

Docker Desktop 4.49.0 uses the `dockerfile_inline` to perform the build. dockerfile_inline is a part of the [Compose Specification](https://github.com/compose-spec/compose-spec/blob/main/build.md#dockerfile_inline).

comments.body: podman-compose [claims support](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/issues/864) for dockerfile_inline, but I don't think that Podman Desktop is using this.

Can you run Docker Compose with Podman as the container engine?

Here's a helpful guide on how to do that: https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/podman-docker-compose

If you start your Podman machine and it prints something like the following, it means the service is correctly listening on the Docker socket:
```
API forwarding listening on: /var/run/docker.sock
Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.
```

You can then run docker-compose commands as usual, and they will automatically use Podman.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@Honny1 Thank you. The instructions provided are for Linux, but I'm using Windows. How do I do this with Windows?

```
> podman machine stop podman-machine-default                                                                                                                                                                            
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully

> podman machine start podman-machine-default                                                                                                                                                                            
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine

Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully

> docker-compose --file docker-compose.yml up
unable to get image 'foo/caddy:latest': error during connect: Get "http://%2F%2F.%2Fpipe%2FdockerDesktopLinuxEngine/v1.51/images/foo/caddy:latest/json": open //./pipe/dockerDesktopLinuxEngine: The system cannot find the file specified.
```

I've never set DOCKER_HOST before so don't know if this is correct but tried the following which seemed to work


I then use podman compose, which still ignored dockerfile_inline and built using the Dockerfile in the folder.

Yes, you did it right. That is exactly what I want you to do. It seems that the issue is with Podman Compose. Can you please report this issue to this repository? https://github.com/containers/podman-compose
